task_type: Engineering Computation & Hypothesis Testing & Open-ended Exploration & Methodology Development & Phenomenological Analysis
task_background: Two-dimensional materials, such as graphene and transition metal dichalcogenides, exhibit unique electronic, optical, and mechanical properties due to their reduced dimensionality.
task_description: Investigate the electronic properties of various two-dimensional materials using computational simulations and experimental data analysis. The goal is to understand how factors such as layer thickness, strain, and external fields influence their behavior, with potential applications in nanoelectronics and optoelectronics.

expected_output.description: Comprehensive report on the electronic properties of 2D materials.
Python code for simulating electronic properties of 2D materials using DFT and molecular dynamics.
Simulation data of electronic properties under various conditions.

input_data.description: Experimental measurements of electronic properties of 2D materials under varying temperature.
